## [WindowBuilder](#)



[tauri](../index.html)::[window](index.html)

# Struct WindowBuilder

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#118-135)

```
/* private fields */
```

Available on **crate feature `unstable`** only.

Expand description

A builder for a window managed by Tauri.

## Implementations[§](#implementations)

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#147-455)[§](#impl-WindowBuilder%3C'a,+R,+M%3E)

### impl<'a, R: [Runtime](../trait.Runtime.html "trait tauri::Runtime"), M: [Manager](../trait.Manager.html "trait tauri::Manager")> [WindowBuilder](struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tauri::window::WindowBuilder")<'a, R, M>

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#209-225)

#### pub fn [new](#method.new)Into<[String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")>>(manager: [&'a M](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html), label: L) -> Self

Initializes a window builder with the given window label.

##### [§](#known-issues)Known issues

On Windows, this function deadlocks when used in a synchronous command or event handlers, see [the Webview2 issue](https://github.com/tauri-apps/wry/issues/583). You should use `async` commands and separate threads when creating windows.

##### [§](#examples)Examples

* Create a window in the setup hook:

```
let "label" ? Ok
```

* Create a window in a separate thread:

```
let move let & "label" Ok
```

* Create a window in a command:

```
#[tauri::command] async fn let & "label"
```

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#256-286)

#### pub fn [from\_config](#method.from_config)(manager: [&'a M](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html), config: &[WindowConfig](https://docs.rs/tauri-utils/2.9.2/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tauri_utils/config/struct.WindowConfig.html "struct tauri_utils::config::WindowConfig")) -> [Result](../type.Result.html "type tauri::Result")

Initializes a window builder from a [`WindowConfig`](https://docs.rs/tauri-utils/2.9.2/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tauri_utils/config/struct.WindowConfig.html "struct tauri_utils::config::WindowConfig") from tauri.conf.json. Keep in mind that you can’t create 2 windows with the same `label` so make sure that the initial window was closed or change the label of the new [`WindowBuilder`](struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tauri::window::WindowBuilder").

##### [§](#known-issues-1)Known issues

On Windows, this function deadlocks when used in a synchronous command or event handlers, see [the Webview2 issue](https://github.com/tauri-apps/wry/issues/583). You should use `async` commands and separate threads when creating windows.

##### [§](#examples-1)Examples

* Create a window in a command:

```
#[tauri::command] async fn let & & 0
```

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#326-332)

#### pub fn [on\_menu\_event](#method.on_menu_event)Fn(&[Window](struct.Window.html "struct tauri::window::Window"), [MenuEvent](../menu/struct.MenuEvent.html "struct tauri::menu::MenuEvent")) + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send") + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync") + 'static>( self, f: F, ) -> Self

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Registers a global menu event listener.

Note that this handler is called for any menu event, whether it is coming from this window, another window or from the tray icon menu.

Also note that this handler will not be called if the window used to register it was closed.

##### [§](#examples-2)Examples

```
use let let "Save" true & ? let & & "File" true & & ? ? let "editor" move if // save menu item /// Ok
```

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#352-354)

#### pub fn [prevent\_overflow](#method.prevent_overflow)(self) -> Self

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Prevent the window from overflowing the working area (e.g. monitor size - taskbar size) on creation, which means the window size will be limited to `monitor size - taskbar size`

**NOTE**: The overflow check is only performed on window creation, resizes can still overflow

###### [§](#platform-specific)Platform-specific

* **iOS / Android:** Unsupported.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#498-503)

#### pub fn [prevent\_overflow\_with\_margin](#method.prevent_overflow_with_margin)(self, margin: impl [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")<[Size](../enum.Size.html "enum tauri::Size")>) -> Self

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Prevent the window from overflowing the working area (e.g. monitor size - taskbar size) on creation with a margin, which means the window size will be limited to `monitor size - taskbar size - margin size`

**NOTE**: The overflow check is only performed on window creation, resizes can still overflow

###### [§](#platform-specific-1)Platform-specific

* **iOS / Android:** Unsupported.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#513-516)

#### pub fn [maximizable](#method.maximizable)(self, maximizable: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> Self

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Whether the window’s native maximize button is enabled or not. If resizable is set to false, this setting is ignored.

###### [§](#platform-specific-2)Platform-specific

* **macOS:** Disables the “zoom” button in the window titlebar, which is also used to enter fullscreen mode.
* **Linux / iOS / Android:** Unsupported.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#524-527)

###### [§](#platform-specific-3)Platform-specific

* **Linux / iOS / Android:** Unsupported.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#537-540)

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Whether the window’s native close button is enabled or not.

###### [§](#platform-specific-4)Platform-specific

* **Linux:** “GTK+ will do its best to convince the window manager not to show a close button. Depending on the system, this function may not have any effect when called on a window that is already visible”
* **iOS / Android:** Unsupported.

#### pub fn [shadow](#method.shadow)(self, enable: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> Self

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Sets whether or not the window has shadow.

###### [§](#platform-specific-7)Platform-specific

* **Windows:**
  + `false` has no effect on decorated window, shadows are always ON.
  + `true` will make undecorated window have a 1px white border, and on Windows 11, it will have a rounded corners.
* **Linux:** Unsupported.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#640-663)

#### pub fn [parent](#method.parent)(self, parent: &[Window](struct.Window.html "struct tauri::window::Window")) -> [Result](../type.Result.html "type tauri::Result")

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Sets a parent to the window to be created.

###### [§](#platform-specific-8)Platform-specific

* **Windows**: This sets the passed parent as an owner window to the window to be created. From [MSDN owned windows docs](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winmsg/window-features#owned-windows):
  + An owned window is always above its owner in the z-order.
  + The system automatically destroys an owned window when its owner is destroyed.
  + An owned window is hidden when its owner is minimized.
* **Linux**: This makes the new window transient for parent, see <https://docs.gtk.org/gtk3/method.Window.set_transient_for.html>
* **macOS**: This adds the window as a child of parent, see <https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nswindow/1419152-addchildwindow?language=objc>

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#734-737)

#### pub fn [transient\_for](#method.transient_for)(self, parent: &[Window](struct.Window.html "struct tauri::window::Window")) -> [Result](../type.Result.html "type tauri::Result")

Available on **`desktop` and (Linux or DragonFly BSD or FreeBSD or NetBSD or OpenBSD)** only.

Sets the window to be created transient for parent.

See <https://docs.gtk.org/gtk3/method.Window.set_transient_for.html>

**Note:** This is a low level API. See [`Self::parent`](struct.WindowBuilder.html#method.parent "method tauri::window::WindowBuilder::parent") for a higher level wrapper for Tauri windows.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#752-755)

#### pub fn [transient\_for\_raw](#method.transient_for_raw)(self, parent: &impl [IsA](https://docs.rs/glib/0.18.5/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/glib/object/trait.IsA.html "trait glib::object::IsA")<[Window](https://docs.rs/gtk/0.18.2/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/gtk/auto/window/struct.Window.html "struct gtk::auto::window::Window")>) -> Self

Available on **`desktop` and (Linux or DragonFly BSD or FreeBSD or NetBSD or OpenBSD)** only.

Sets the window to be created transient for parent.

See <https://docs.gtk.org/gtk3/method.Window.set_transient_for.html>

**Note:** This is a low level API. See [`Self::parent`](struct.WindowBuilder.html#method.parent "method tauri::window::WindowBuilder::parent") and [`Self::transient_for`](struct.WindowBuilder.html#method.transient_for "method tauri::window::WindowBuilder::transient_for") for higher level wrappers for Tauri windows.

[Source](../../src/tauri/window/mod.rs.html#802-805)

#### pub fn [effects](#method.effects)(self, effects: [WindowEffectsConfig](https://docs.rs/tauri-utils/2.9.2/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tauri_utils/config/struct.WindowEffectsConfig.html "struct tauri_utils::config::WindowEffectsConfig")) -> Self

Available on **`desktop`** only.

Sets window effects.

Requires the window to be transparent.

* **Linux**: Unsupported
